1925	\begin{proof}
1926	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1927	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1928	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1929	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1930	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1931	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1932	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
1933	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
1934	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
1935	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
1936	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
1937	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
1938	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
1939	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
1940	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
1941	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
1942	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
1943	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
1944	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
1945	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1946	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
1947	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
1948	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
1949	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
1950	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
1951	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
1952	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
1953	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
1954	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
1955	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
1956	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
1957	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
1958	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
1959	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
1960	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1961	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
1962	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
1963	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
1964	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
1965	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
1966	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
1967	
1968	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
1969	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
1970	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
1971	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
1972	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
1973	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
1974	
1975	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
1976	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
1977	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
1978	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
1979	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
1980	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
1981	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
1982	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
1983	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
1984	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1985	\end{proof}

**Key findings:**

1. **must-fix-this-iter — Blueprint proof-method contradiction on `tensorObj_assoc_iso`**: The blueprint (rewritten this iter) describes "direct gluing via `tensorobj_restrict_iso`, no whiskering." The Lean proof uses `W_whiskerRight_of_W`/`W_whiskerLeft_of_W` (route-d three-step composite) — exactly the whiskering it says is absent. These are mathematically different strategies.

The make-or-break finding is confirmed by the Lean code: `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` calls `tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N` on ARBITRARY `M N` before any triviality is applied, so H1 is genuinely on the critical path. The blueprint must be corrected to reflect this.

3. **major** — The 5 substantive iter-216 declarations (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ`, `restrictScalars_isIso_ε`, `restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv`, and the `_of_bijective` variants) have no `\lean{...}` pins. The blueprint's `lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv` block pins only `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` and doesn't name the strong-monoidal packaging needed for the presheaf lift.
